title: agnos.json
description: Single source of truth for an agnos project: which agents are active, where rules live, what skills are installed, which MCP servers are configured, and how docs are managed.

$schema.description: URL of this JSON schema. Recommended so editors can validate and autocomplete agnos.json.

agents.description: Active agent plugins, by id (e.g. "claude-code") or by full npm package name (e.g. "@me/agnos-agent-zed") when disambiguating colliding ids.

rules.description: Where canonical rule files live. Each active agent materializes its own rule artifacts (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, etc.) by mirroring every canonical file. Supports nested rule files across the directory tree.

filename.description: Canonical basename for every rule file (default "AGENTS.md"). Keep it agent-neutral — do not set it to an active agent's own output name.

root.description: Base directory for canonical rule sources, project-relative (default "."). The root rule file is <root>/<filename>. May differ from where agents read, so sources can live in e.g. ./docs while agents see mirrors at their real locations.

dirs.description: Additional directories (relative to root) that each hold a <filename>. May contain "..". Defaults to [] (a single root rule file).

skills.description: Skills domain configuration: where canonical content lives and which skills to install.

route.description: Canonical skills directory, project-relative. Defaults to ".agnos/skills". Agents symlink their own skills dir to this path.

sources.description: Map of local skill name → composite source ref. Grammar: "github:<owner>/<repo>/<in-repo-path>" (or gitlab:/bitbucket:), or "file:<path-to-skill-dir>" pointing at a directory containing SKILL.md.

mcp.description: MCP server declarations. Each entry is materialized into per-agent MCP config (e.g. .mcp.json for Claude Code, .codex/config.toml for Codex).

name.description: Local name for this MCP server.

source.description: Optional source ref for the server package.

env.description: Environment variables for the server process. Values may contain ${VAR} references resolved at materialization time.

transport.description: Transport protocol the agent uses to talk to this server.

hooks.description: Hooks registry, keyed by hook event name (e.g. "PreToolUse", "SessionStart"). Modeled on Claude Code's hook shape (the superset across agents) and intentionally permissive: a handler only needs a `type`, and unknown fields pass through so agent dialects survive. Each agent materializes this into its own format/location: Claude Code → .claude/settings.json#hooks, Codex → .codex/hooks.json (filtered to its supported events and command handlers).

propertyNames.description: Hook event name. Shared across agents: SessionStart, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PermissionRequest, PreCompact, PostCompact, UserPromptSubmit, SubagentStart, SubagentStop, Stop. Claude Code adds many more (e.g. Notification, FileChanged, SessionEnd) which Codex ignores.

matcher.description: Filter for when the hooks fire. What it matches depends on the event: tool name (PreToolUse/PostToolUse/PermissionRequest), source (SessionStart), or trigger (PreCompact). "", "*", or omitted matches all; letters/digits/`_`/`|` are an exact or pipe-separated list; anything else is a regex.

hooks.description: Hook handlers run when the matcher fires.

type.description: Handler type. "command" is the only type portable to every agent. Claude Code also supports "http", "mcp_tool", "prompt", and "agent"; Codex supports "command" only and drops the rest.

command.description: For command hooks: the executable or shell line to run. Supports ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR} and other placeholders on agents that resolve them.

args.description: For command hooks: arguments passed in exec form (no shell interpretation). Omit to run `command` through a shell.

timeout.description: Seconds before the handler is cancelled (default 600 on both agents).

statusMessage.description: Optional UI status text shown while the handler runs.

docs.description: Docs domain configuration. Controls where documentation lives, what frontmatter metadata is required, and whether the rules/index blocks get injected back into the rules file.

metadata.description: Map of frontmatter key → natural-language description. Tells the LLM how to populate each value when generating or updating a doc.

index.description: Basename of the generated docs index file (default "index").

content.description: Basename of the content doc, or `false` to skip generating one (default "content").

injectIndex.description: Inject the documentation index block into the rules file (default true).

injectRules.description: Inject the documentation-rules block into the rules file (default true).
